title: The shoe-shiner's notebook
summary: For thirty-one years, a shoe-shiner in Buenos Aires has written down every person who sat in his chair.
body:
Mr Diego shines shoes in Buenos Aires. He works on a busy corner. His chair is wooden. He has a small box of brushes and creams.

He has worked here for thirty-one years.

Diego is not famous. The corner is not famous. But Diego keeps a notebook. He writes down every person who sits in his chair.

He writes the date. He writes a short description: 'old man, grey hat.' 'Young woman, blue dress, late for a meeting.' 'Boy, his first time, his father paid.' He does not write names.

He has filled twenty-three notebooks.

A university student saw the notebooks one day. She studied history. She asked, 'Can I read them? This is a history of the corner.'

Diego said, 'It is not history. It is shoes.'

The student laughed. 'No. It is also history. Twenty-three notebooks. Three decades. Every person who walked here.'

Diego thought about it. He said, 'I started because I was bored. The first day, I wrote one person. The next day, two more. After a year, I could not stop.'

'Why did you not stop?'

'Because then a person would sit in my chair and I would not write them. And I would feel I did not see them. I clean shoes. But I think seeing the person is also the work.'

The student asked if she could photograph one page.

Diego said, 'Photograph the page from June 1998. That was a very good day. Sixteen customers. The corner smelled of rain.'

annotations.meaningVi: lời mô tả / sự mô tả
lịch sử / câu chuyện theo thời gian
có mùi của (cụm: smell of + danh từ)

grammarNotes.title: Reported question with 'if'
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Khi kể lại câu hỏi yes/no, tiếng Anh dùng 'asked if + mệnh đề'. Trong bài: 'The student asked if she could photograph one page.' Câu gốc là 'Can I photograph one page?' Khi kể lại, 'can' thành 'could' và bỏ dấu chấm hỏi.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: To report a yes/no question, English uses 'asked if + clause'. From the passage: 'The student asked if she could photograph one page.' The original question was 'Can I photograph one page?' When reported, 'can' becomes 'could' and the question mark goes away.

translationVi:
Ông Diego đánh giày ở Buenos Aires. Ông làm ở một góc phố đông. Ghế của ông bằng gỗ. Ông có một cái hộp nhỏ đựng bàn chải và kem.

Ông đã làm ở đây ba mươi mốt năm.

Diego không nổi tiếng. Góc phố cũng không. Nhưng Diego có một cuốn sổ. Ông ghi lại mỗi người ngồi vào ghế của ông.

Ông ghi ngày. Ông viết một dòng mô tả ngắn: 'ông già, mũ xám.' 'Cô gái trẻ, váy xanh, muộn giờ họp.' 'Cậu bé, lần đầu, cha trả tiền.' Ông không ghi tên.

Ông đã viết kín hai mươi ba cuốn sổ.

Một nữ sinh đại học thấy mấy cuốn sổ một hôm. Cô học lịch sử. Cô hỏi: 'Cháu đọc được không ạ? Đây là lịch sử của góc phố này.'

Diego nói: 'Không phải lịch sử. Là giày.'

Cô nữ sinh cười. 'Không. Cũng là lịch sử nữa. Hai mươi ba cuốn. Ba thập kỷ. Mỗi người đi qua đây.'

Diego suy nghĩ. Ông nói: 'Tôi bắt đầu vì chán. Ngày đầu, tôi viết một người. Ngày sau, hai người nữa. Sau một năm, tôi không dừng được.'

'Sao ông không dừng?'

'Vì lúc đó sẽ có người ngồi vào ghế tôi mà tôi không viết về họ. Và tôi sẽ cảm thấy mình không nhìn họ. Tôi lau giày. Nhưng tôi nghĩ nhìn thấy con người cũng là phần công việc.'

Cô nữ sinh hỏi cô có thể chụp một trang không.

Diego nói: 'Chụp trang tháng Sáu năm 1998 đi. Hôm đó là một ngày rất đẹp. Mười sáu khách. Góc phố thơm mùi mưa.'

options: A busy corner in Buenos Aires
questions.explanation: 'Mr Diego shines shoes in Buenos Aires. He works on a busy corner.'

questions.prompt: How long has he worked at this corner?
questions.explanation: 'He has worked here for thirty-one years.'

questions.prompt: What does he write in his notebook?
options: The date and a short description of each customer (no names)
Each customer's full name and address
questions.explanation: 'He writes the date. He writes a short description… He does not write names.'

questions.prompt: How many notebooks has he filled?
Who took an interest in the notebooks?
questions.explanation: 'A university student saw the notebooks one day. She studied history.'

questions.prompt: Why did Diego start the notebook?
options: Because he wanted to be famous
Because then he would feel he did not really see the person sitting in his chair
Because his daughter wants the records
Because the city pays for it
questions.explanation: 'Because then a person would sit in my chair and I would not write them. And I would feel I did not see them.'

questions.prompt: What does Diego say about his work?
options: 'I clean shoes. But I think seeing the person is also the work.'

questions.prompt: Which page does Diego suggest the student photograph?
options: June 1998 — sixteen customers and the corner smelled of rain
A simple act of attention, repeated for decades, becomes its own kind of history
Universities should pay more attention to street workers
Buenos Aires has too many notebooks
questions.explanation: The student names this and Diego accepts it.
questions.hint: Think about the student's phrase 'a history of the corner'.

blanks.explanation: He started because he was bored.
Seeing the person is also the work.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Diego writes a short description of every customer because he believes 'seeing the person is also the work.' If you kept a one-line record of everyone you served or interacted with this week, what would the page look like — and would your view of your own job change?
